title: horkelia cuneata var. puberula
intro: Horkelia cuneata var. puberula, also known as the hairy-leafed horkelia, is a perennial wildflower native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and salves, which are said to have health benefits like soothing sore throats and relieving digestive issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effectiveness and potential interactions of this plant, and it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
